page: Amazon Web Services m8in.4xlarge is a general-purpose virtual machine instance optimized for network and EBS performance. It is powered by an Intel x86_64 processor operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance provides 16 virtual CPUs (vCPUs) mapped to 8 physical cores with 2 threads per core under a dedicated CPU allocation model. It is equipped with 64.0 GB of system memory, which translates to 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core.

Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage, requiring all data persistence to be managed via network-attached Elastic Block Store (EBS) volumes. It features a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps to support high-throughput data transfers. The instance does not include graphics processing units (GPUs) or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the m8in.4xlarge offers a balanced ratio of compute to memory, making it a cost-efficient option for workloads that require dedicated CPU resources without the higher cost of memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated instances. The primary tradeoff is its reliance on network-attached storage, which may introduce latency compared to local NVMe drives. This configuration is suitable for general-purpose workloads such as web serving, application servers, containerized microservices, and medium-sized database hosting.
description: Amazon Web Services m8in.4xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized instance built on the Nitro hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel x86_64 processor running at 3.9 GHz, alongside 64.0 GB of system memory. This configuration provides a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per physical core across its 8 physical cores. The instance lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, relying entirely on network-attached storage for data persistence. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 25 Gbps. This resource profile makes the m8in.4xlarge suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications, web serving, containerized microservices, and database workloads that require stable, dedicated compute performance without the need for local high-speed storage or hardware acceleration.

tagline: A balanced general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Intel processors, high-speed network optimization, and a stable compute-to-memory ratio for enterprise workloads.
